title: D5/D6/D7: grooming learns to see bundle rot; planning and compounding stop misrouting
action: Three skills, three narrow corrections. (D5, the substantive one) `skills/bee-grooming/SKILL.md` contains `docs/knowledge` ZERO times and `docs/specs` zero times, yet its entropy score counts 'un-synced specs'/'stale specs' — so the one skill whose job is finding rot is blind to the layer that now holds the truth. Give its debt hunt a bundle dimension that uses ONLY what the bundle can already prove mechanically: the conformance check's own findings (`node .bee/bin/bee.mjs knowledge check --json` — its errors, profile errors and warnings, including dangling required_context/supersedes targets and duplicate authority claims) and areas that have code but no concept. Never a hand-maintained list of areas, and never a new verb: `knowledge stale` is backlog P68 and explicitly out of scope. Keep grooming's existing project-language reporting rule intact (bee's own housekeeping stays a short side-note; `.bee/`, `.claude/`, `.codex/` are never project debt). The bundle dimension must be gated on bundle mode, so a never-migrated host's grooming run is unchanged. (D6) `skills/bee-planning/SKILL.md`: its standard/high-risk bootstrap already names the knowledge-context command; its tiny/small bootstrap names only the critical-patterns digest and CONTEXT.md. Add the same knowledge-context mention to the tiny/small bootstrap, conditioned exactly as the standard/high-risk one is (when the active feature has a work item). Lanes scale ceremony, never the context an agent is allowed to have. (D7) `skills/bee-compounding/SKILL.md`: the State-Layer-Guard prose reads that `bee-scribing` owns `docs/specs/`. The mechanical check it describes (reading the scribing record from state) is already layer-agnostic and still correct — only the prose misdescribes what is owned. Correct it to the state layer generically (the bundle when the repo has one, else the spec tree), and check the same file's reference section for the same stale framing.

truths: grep -c 'knowledge' on bee-grooming/SKILL.md returns > 0 and the added dimension names a MECHANICAL source (knowledge check findings + areas with code and no concept), not a hand list
the grooming bundle dimension is gated on bundle mode — a never-migrated host's grooming run is described as unchanged
bee-planning's tiny/small bootstrap names the knowledge-context command under the same condition the standard/high-risk bootstrap uses
no line in bee-compounding/SKILL.md still says bee-scribing owns docs/specs/ as the state layer
prohibitions: do not invent a `knowledge stale` verb or any new CLI surface — out of scope, backlog P68
do not make grooming report bee's own internals as project debt (its existing rule)
no new content written under docs/specs/ (D8)
do NOT regenerate the plugin trees, the release manifest, the .bee/bin mirror or the onboarding ledger — f4-3 owns all four for the whole feature (D9)

trace.outcome: D5/D6/D7 closed. Grooming can now see bundle rot: its debt hunt branches on bundle mode and counts only what the bundle already proves mechanically — the conformance check's own findings (dangling required_context/supersedes, duplicate ids and authority claims, OKF and profile errors) plus areas with code and no concept — never a hand list and without inventing the knowledge-stale verb that is backlog P68. Planning's tiny/small bootstraps now name the knowledge-context command under the same condition the bigger lanes use, so the smaller lanes stop being denied context. Compounding's prose no longer claims scribing owns docs/specs/; it owns the state layer, bundle or specs, which is what its already-layer-agnostic check actually verifies.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "BEFORE (measured on HEAD): skills/bee-grooming/SKILL.md contained 'docs/knowledge' 0 times AND 'docs/specs' 0 times, while its entropy score counted 'un-synced specs' / 'stale specs' — so the one skill whose entire job is finding rot could not see the layer that now holds the truth. A bundle with dangling required_context targets, duplicate authority claims, or an area with code and no concept would have been invisible to every grooming run, forever, with no suite red. Separately: bee-planning's tiny/small bootstrap named only the critical-patterns digest and CONTEXT.md while the standard/high-risk bootstrap already named the knowledge-context command — the smaller lanes were denied context the larger ones get, which inverts 'lanes scale ceremony, never memory'. And bee-compounding's State-Layer-Guard prose asserted 'bee-scribing owns docs/specs/' in three places, which stopped being true when scribing became bundle-first.",
  "after": "AFTER: grooming's debt hunt branches on bundle mode and names only MECHANICAL sources — the conformance check's own JSON output (OKF errors, profile errors and warnings, dangling required_context/supersedes, duplicate bee.id and bee.authoritative_for) plus areas with code and no concept via the existing capped-cell-to-area mapping. No hand-maintained list, and no new verb invented (knowledge stale is backlog P68, explicitly out of scope). The no-bundle branch is stated as unchanged. bee-planning's tiny and small bootstraps now name the knowledge-context command under the identical condition the standard/high-risk bootstrap uses. bee-compounding's ownership line and its two Red-Flags echoes now read 'the state layer — docs/knowledge/ when the repo has a bundle, else docs/specs/'; the mechanical check itself was already layer-agnostic and was left untouched.",
  "worker_grounding": "The worker ran `bee knowledge check --json` against the live bundle and read the command registry's own description of the finding codes BEFORE writing grooming's new dimension, so the skill describes findings the check actually produces rather than plausible-sounding ones.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Diff read in full by the orchestrator. Scope respected: 5 files, all inside the cell's declared surface, none overlapping the sibling cell's two reference files. The mechanical check in bee-compounding was correctly left alone — only its prose was wrong.",
  "chain": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS 79 suite(s), exit 0, run SERIALLY by the orchestrator after both sibling workers were off the tree and after the plugin-tree render + release-manifest write.",
  "transient_red_resolved": "Both workers reported test_guards.mjs and/or test_hook_contracts.mjs red mid-swarm; confirmed transient (standalone and full re-runs pass, and the orchestrator's own serial full-chain run is green on all 79). Two workers were running the full chain concurrently — the known 'a shared suite red is not yours while another agent is mid-write' pattern."
}
